raw_input:
[5.0/5] Best shaver ever Any Braun is the way to go. Best shaver ever, all of them. [4.0/5] Not bad After reading a lot of reviews i decided to go for it.
Overall i am happy with it, the only 'cons' are:
- have to cover an area a lot of times to get a really smooth shave. A 'quick shave' is simply not an option.
- it pulls on longer hairs. Best to use the trimmer first if there are long hairs or you are in for some pain.

Pro's:
- once done, the shave is incredibly smooth
- love the trimmer
- no burn, the adjustable speed really helps
- cleaning is a breeze
- battery life and charging speed are great [1.0/5] BACK TO PANASONIC SEVERAL YEARS AGO I HAD A BRAUN I LIKED VERY WELL. SO I THOUGHT THIS NEW SERIES 7 WOULD BE THE LATEST AND GREATEST. I COULD GET A BETTER SHAVE WITH A JOHN DEERE COMBINE. LAST YEAR I BOUGHT A 4 HEAD PANASONIC NANO TECHNOLOGY ETC AND SHAVES OK BUT IT IS VERY CLUMSY AND YOU HAVE TO WORK TO GET A GOOD SHAVE. I HAVE TRIED NORELCO ALSO AND SENT IT BACK AFTER 90 DAYS. I WENT BACK TO THE BEST RAZOR I HAVE EVER OWNED, A PANASONIC ES 8109, IT IS A 3 HEAD NANO TECHNOLOGY ETC. BEST SHAVE EVER AND YOU DON'T HAVE TO WORK AT IT. MY OLD ONE FINALLY GAVE OUT AFTER MANY GOOD YEARS. [5.0/5] great shaver This shaver works great. Close shave. No irritation. Simple. I doubt I will ever go back to using a blade. [5.0/5] great shaving experience Braun 7-790cc: Very gentle, fast, and close shave. Compared with my old shaver (Philishave HQ7415) 10x less skin irritation and 3x faster. 5 stars for the shave. Positive surprise: You can charge the shaver without the cleaning station, only with the cord, and also use it with the cord when it is not charged. Disadvantages: a) Cleaning with tap water is clearly more tedious than for simpler shavers (like my old Philips). b) Doesn't bother me, might bother others: It is louder than other shavers. c) "Hidden" costs: 5 USD/month for the cleaning cardridges and from the 19th month of use on additional 2 USD/month for the replacement shaving unit when everything is used as recommended. But compare the high cost for Gilette Sensor blades plus foam! d) Pure cosmetics (but if you buy the Mercedes you want perfect design): The lowest bar indicating battery charge has a red frame, so red = bad. At the same time, the best bar for hygiene status has a red frame as well, so here red = optimum. Doesn't make sense. In addition the green LEDs on the cleaning station have some cross-talk. If one is on, you can think two are on, at least in a dark bathroom as some light is bleeding through... [5.0/5] Great Shaver This replaced a 12 year old Braun shaver. All I can say is wow. Best and most comfortable shaves ever. [5.0/5] Braun Series 7 This was a Xmas gift for my son. He absolutely loves it. When he first used it, he said that he was getting razor burn, but as he got use to it, that went away and he now gets the best shave he has ever had. [5.0/5] Best Shaver Just Got Better! Okay, so the title of the review is a little excessive, but I really do love this shaver. I used a older Braun 6-series for 5-6 years before it died, I think it may have died because I wasn't thinking and I used it in the shower. Then again, maybe it was a subliminal attempt to get myself a new one for Christmas! While I understand that the improvements are said to be slight over Braun's last flagship model, the difference is drastic and noticeable over my older model. I have a fairly sensitive face, when it comes to shaving, and the old Braun, while it gave me the best electric shave I had experienced, tended to occasionally pull a bit and would miss a hair or two. This new Braun is much, much smoother in its shave. It has not pulled at all. I love that it has three power settings. This 790 rarely misses a hair and is clearly a closer shave. The head of the shaver has more mobility, which is a big plus for someone like me who has a very angular and chiseled face reminiscent of the gods of Olympus... just kidding. Though, it is a significant improvement from the older models and a handy feature. The rubbered grip is also a big improvement, as the old one could be a little slippery at times. I prefer the new travel case as well. The old one was plastic and snapped shut, and it always popped open in my bag when I traveled. The new case is zippered and is a firm-formed material, which makes it much better for travel. The digital display on the shaver handle, showing battery life and "hygiene," is much more informative than the old display and is, again, very useful in travel situations. It is much more clear in the documentation of this model that you can clean the head with water, again, good for travel. I really like the new design for the replaceable foils. The old foils broke apart into 2-3 pieces, which made it a little more inconvenient for cleaning and maintaining the sharpness of the blades. This new foil is one piece and is virtually entirely enclosed, it would be hard to quantify, but the design seems to allow for a better cleaning through the base station. Finally, the base station is different from the old one. It is slightly less intuitive. I had to make sure I read the manual to make sure I understood what various flashing lights meant, as opposed to solid lights. The cleaning process was much more clear with the old model, you put it in the base station, clicked the button into lock and the shaver said "cleaning," and displayed the amount of time remaining. The new base station does not display this kind of information, instead the manual explains the various cleaning times. Getting it to start the cleaning is a little less straight-forward, and you can guess how much cleaning time is left by observing the rate at which the "hygiene" bars replenish. One really good thing about the new base station, is that it seems to go to sleep after being unused for a while. It wakes up upon pushing a button or replacing the shaver, this is a big plus because it saves energy and that is always good. The long and short of this review is that I love this shaver. I loved my old Braun, but this new one makes me wonder how I put up with the old one for so long. [4.0/5] Smooth as ever. Despite of what some people have complained about, the new 790cc-4 is many times better than my old Braun 7075 Syncro model. The first thing I noticed is how SMOOTH and COMFORTABLE it shaves, comparing to the Syncro, not to mention the shaving head no longer heats up like before. The Syncro used to occasionally cut my face at difficult to reach places, such as under the nose and by the neck, because I had to do several passes until it would shave properly. The 790cc-4 requires less passes and feels *extremely* comfortable in comparison. This also means I now shave faster than ever, and this used to be hassle to me. The body shape has changed but I still find it easy to hold, so it's fine with me.

But there were some things I didn't like:

* The sideburns cutter is at an awkward angle and make it difficult to see what I am doing. It also has it's own button now, which used to be the same as the power one in older models. I don't think this is an improvement.

* Locking the shaving head now has a separate button that is difficult to reach, and requires me to use both hands to do it. It looks rather flimsy, so I try not to use it much, afraid that I am going to break it - one person claimed to have already broken it in another review. Locking the head used to be simple with older models, so again, this was not an improvement.

* The base is now "automated", meaning that it decides on its own when I should buy a new cleaning solution cartridge. The buttons become unresponsive - it will simply stop working until you do it. Last time this happened, the cartridge was still a quarter full but the base would not let me use it anymore. Again, I don't think this is an improvement - it should let me decide.

* The shaver has built-in battery and "hygiene" displays, which may sound good at a first glance. The hygiene one measures (I assume) how long you took to shave, and then uses that information to tell the base for how long it will keep cleaning it. The older models would simply run a cleaning cycle and get done with it, while the new 790cc-4 wants to make it longer if I took longer to shave. This may sound like wasting cleaning solution when the amount of shaved hair from my face will not increase if I take longer to shave, so therefore it shouldn't take longer to clean. Sounds gimmicky to me, and perhaps makes you buy new cleaning solution cartridges more often?

* The shaving head has (so far) been impossible to find at local stores. From this I could assume that this shaver is not selling much, and that worries me because it wasn't cheap and I plan to use it for years to come. Braun claims the blades should last for 18 months of regular use, but my beard is so hard that they don't last even 10 months. Lack of offer might also mean that the head will remain expensive for lack of competition. Major stores here in Chicago (Target, Sears, etc) are not selling this shaver, so it makes sense they don't sell the shaving head, and that kinda worries me.

* The power button is tiny and hard to reach. The button sits at my thumb's 1st knuckle, and that's what I have to use to reach it. Used to be easy to turn those shavers on and off with the older models, so this was not an improvement.

* The manual suggests I only shave before taking a bath, which in my personal experience produces the worst shaving ever. People who have posted negative reviews here may have fallen for this. Shaving about 10 minutes *after* a bath works like a dream to me, and also causes less skin irritation.

Despite the rather questionable "improvements", I still like this shaver a lot and will not ever come back to my older Syncro - even because Braun has discontinued production of its shaving head, so I can't buy it anymore. So in a way, Braun has forced me to buy this new one and I don't regret the money I have spent on it. For what matters, it shaves closer and smoother than anything I tried before. Some people have complained that it is too noisy, but I find it much quieter than my older syncro. Conversely, the cleaning base is actually louder, but that doesn't matter to me.

All in all, I strongly recommend the 790cc-4 to anybody who can afford it. I took a star down for the issues above, but those do not keep me from having a perfect shave, faster and smoother than ever. [5.0/5] Question I just received my razor and I noticed it does not come with a clear plastic cover to protect the foil as my previous razors have is it missing?? [5.0/5] Works excellent shaving with a regular blade -- no matter the blade, always gives me red bumps and ingrown hairs. I am able to get just as close a shave with this razor with none of the bumps or ingrown hairs.

I would recommend this to anyone thinking about buying it. Although the lesser model's I imagine work similarly well.

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

The Braun Series 7 790cc electric shaver receives mostly positive reviews, with many customers rating it 5 out of 5 stars. Common praise includes its close and smooth shave, low skin irritation, good battery life, easy cleaning, and useful trimmer. Several reviewers noted it was a significant improvement over older Braun models and other brands like Philips and Norelco. People with sensitive skin particularly appreciated the reduced irritation compared to manual razors or older electric shavers.

Some criticisms were noted in the lower-rated reviews. These included the shaver sometimes struggling with longer hairs, an awkward sideburn trimmer angle, a small and hard-to-reach power button, a locking head mechanism that felt flimsy, and the automated cleaning base that forces users to buy cleaning cartridges. The ongoing cost of cleaning cartridges and replacement shaving heads was also a concern for some buyers, as replacement heads can be hard to find in local stores.

One reviewer strongly preferred a Panasonic shaver over the Braun, while another noted a missing plastic foil cover upon receiving the product. Overall, the majority of reviewers were very satisfied with the shaver and would recommend it, particularly for those looking for a comfortable, close shave with minimal skin irritation.
